title: September Woodlands.
content:
This is not sadness in the wood;
    The yellowbird
    Flits joying through the solitude,
    By no thought stirred
    Save of his little duskier mate
    And rompings jolly.

    If there's a Dryad in the wood,
    She is not sad.
    Too wise the spirits are to brood;
    Divinely glad,
    They dream with countenance sedate
    Not melancholy.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Bliss Carman (William) delivers a powerful performance in "September Woodlands."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
